what: The airtight durable-first-paint proof for examples/live-capstone (evidence task L-aa9779f5). Captured via Playwright against the live `bun run serve` (dev SQLite-poll path).
method: Reloaded the app with BOTH server data routes intercepted and aborted at the network layer (page.route → route.abort), then read the rendered #messages list. With the wire provably unreachable, any rendered row can only have come from the durable OPFS-SQLite store.

rowsRenderedFromOpfs: alice: hello from alice — durable OPFS proof
alice: offline write by alice — queued in the outbox
alice: after failover — this tab was promoted to leader
result.statusLine: alice @ lobby — ready — 0 message(s)  (NB: the '0' is the init-time snapshot count read BEFORE the async leader store attaches — the list is the authoritative render; see evidence/README.md boundary #2)
result.networkFailureObserved: net::ERR_FAILED (the SSE subscription could not reach the server)

conclusion: The /__lesto/live-data subscription failed (net::ERR_FAILED) yet all 3 rows still painted, so the durable OPFS store — not the wire — is the source. This is the proof the screenshot alone cannot carry (a screenshot of a reload looks identical whether the wire was blocked or not); the aborted-request record is the load-bearing artifact.
premises: Single tab open (no second tab whose BroadcastChannel snapshot could paint a follower with zero network).
No service worker ships in this example (so no cached app-data response could substitute for the wire).
Both /__lesto/live-data and /messages were intercepted, covering the SSE subscription and any REST read.
